title: Email notifications delayed 2-4 hours since ~v2.3.1 update (likely related to daily digest feature)
problem: Task assignment email notifications that previously arrived within 1-2 minutes are now delayed by 2-4 hours. The delay is worse for notifications sent in the morning (9-10am) and somewhat shorter in the afternoon. All team members are affected regardless of mail client.
root_cause_hypothesis: The daily digest feature introduced around a week ago likely changed email notification queuing or batching behavior. Morning notifications may be getting caught in a digest aggregation window or a queue that builds up overnight and drains slowly. The timing correlation between the feature rollout and the onset of delays strongly suggests the digest feature is either batching notifications that should be sent immediately, or its background processing is competing with real-time notification delivery.
reproduction_steps: Use a self-hosted TaskFlow v2.3.1 instance with SMTP relay configured
Assign a task to a user around 9-10am
Observe that the email notification arrives 2-4 hours later instead of within 1-2 minutes
Repeat in the afternoon and observe a shorter but still present delay
environment: TaskFlow v2.3.1, self-hosted instance, email delivered via corporate SMTP relay, recipients using Outlook (but issue is client-independent)
impact: Entire team is affected. Delayed notifications cause missed deadlines because assignees don't learn about tasks for hours. Business-critical workflow disruption.
recommended_fix: Investigate the daily digest feature's interaction with real-time notifications. Check whether: (1) the notification queue or scheduler was modified to batch emails for digest purposes, (2) real-time notifications are being routed through the digest pipeline instead of the immediate-send path, (3) a background job for digest processing is blocking or starving the real-time notification worker. Review the email sending code changes introduced with the digest feature and verify that immediate notifications bypass digest batching entirely.
proposed_test_case: With the daily digest feature enabled, assign a task and verify that the assignment notification email is delivered within 2 minutes. Repeat at multiple times of day (morning and afternoon) to confirm no time-of-day regression. Also verify that digest emails are generated separately and do not interfere with real-time notification delivery.
information_gaps: Server-side email queue logs showing where notifications are dwelling
Exact SMTP relay configuration and whether any relay-side throttling exists
Whether the daily digest feature is explicitly enabled for this instance or was auto-enabled by the update
Whether disabling the digest feature resolves the delay (quick validation of hypothesis)
